terms.definition: The useful substances in food that our body needs to stay healthy, grow, and get energy.
terms.exampleUsage: Fruits and vegetables are full of important nutrients like vitamins.

terms.definition: A meal that has the right amounts of all the different types of food our body needs.
terms.exampleUsage: A thali with roti, dal, sabzi, rice, and curd is an example of a balanced diet.

terms.definition: Nutrients in food that give us energy. Found in foods like rice, roti, bread, and potatoes.
terms.exampleUsage: We get carbohydrates from the chapati we eat for lunch.

terms.definition: Nutrients that help our body grow and repair itself. Found in dal, milk, eggs, and paneer.
terms.exampleUsage: Children need proteins from foods like dal and milk to grow strong.

terms.definition: Nutrients found in fruits and vegetables that protect our body from diseases.
terms.exampleUsage: Oranges and amla are rich in Vitamin C, which protects us from colds.

terms.definition: Nutrients needed in small amounts for strong bones, teeth, and blood. Found in green vegetables and milk.
terms.exampleUsage: Calcium is a mineral found in milk that makes our bones strong.

terms.definition: Food that tastes good but has very little nutrition and lots of oil, sugar, or salt.
terms.exampleUsage: Chips, cold drinks, and candy are examples of junk food we should eat less of.

terms.definition: The rough part of food from plants that helps our stomach work properly and digest food.
terms.exampleUsage: Eating whole wheat roti and fruits gives us fibre for good digestion.

terms.definition: The power our body gets from food to do all activities like walking, running, thinking, and playing.
terms.exampleUsage: We need energy from food to play cricket and study for exams.

terms.definition: Keeping ourselves and our food clean to stay healthy and avoid getting sick.
terms.exampleUsage: Washing hands before eating is an important part of food hygiene.
